items.headline: Always-on detections: eliminating the WAF “log versus block” trade-off

content.excerpt_text: Cloudflare is introducing Attack Signature Detection and Full-Transaction Detection to provide continuous, high-fidelity security insights without the manual tuning of traditional WAFs. By correlating request payloads with server responses, we can now identify successful exploits and data exfiltration while minimizing false positives.

items.headline: Mind the gap: new tools for continuous enforcement from boot to login

content.excerpt_text: Cloudflare’s mandatory authentication and independent MFA protect organizations by ensuring continuous enforcement, from the moment a machine boots until sensitive resources are accessed.

items.headline: Defeating the deepfake: stopping laptop farms and insider threats

content.excerpt_text: Cloudflare One is partnering with Nametag to combat laptop farms and AI-enhanced identity fraud by requiring identity verification during employee onboarding and via continuous authentication.

items.headline: Moving from license plates to badges: the Gateway Authorization Proxy

content.excerpt_text: Cloudflare’s Gateway Authorization Proxy adds support for identity-aware policies for clientless devices, securing virtual desktops, and guest networks without a device client.

items.headline: Stop reacting to breaches and start preventing them with User Risk Scoring

content.excerpt_text: Cloudflare One now incorporates dynamic User Risk Scores into Access policies to enable automated, adaptive security responses. This update allows teams to move beyond binary "allow/deny" rules by evaluating continuous behavior signals from both internal and third-party sources.

items.headline: Introducing the 2026 Cloudflare Threat Report

content.excerpt_text: There has been a fundamental shift toward industrialized cyber threats, highlighted by a record 31.4 Tbps DDoS attack and sophisticated session token theft. Our new report examines how nation-states and criminal actors have moved beyond traditional exploits to "living off the XaaS" within legitimate enterprise logic.

items.headline: Evolving Cloudflare’s Threat Intelligence Platform: actionable, scalable, and ETL-less

content.excerpt_text: Stop managing ETL pipelines and start threat hunting. Introducing new visualization, automation, and enrichment tools in the Cloudflare Threat Intelligence Platform to turn massive telemetry into instant security posture.

items.headline: How Cloudy translates complex security into human action

content.excerpt_text: Cloudy is our LLM-powered explanation layer built directly into Cloudflare One. Its explanations, now part of Phishnet and API CASB, can improve user decisions and SOC efficiency.

items.headline: From reactive to proactive: closing the phishing gap with LLMs

content.excerpt_text: Email security is a constant arms race. Like WWII engineers reinforcing only the planes that returned, survivorship bias hides real gaps. But LLMs can help us find the invisible weaknesses.

items.headline: See risk, fix risk: introducing Remediation in Cloudflare CASB

content.excerpt_text: Cloudflare CASB Remediation lets security teams go beyond visibility to fix risky file sharing in Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace directly from Cloudflare One, all in just a few clicks.

items.headline: Modernizing with agile SASE: a Cloudflare One blog takeover

content.excerpt_text: In 2026, agile SASE is the engine for modernization. Discover how Cloudflare One secures humans, devices, and AI agents on a single, programmable connectivity cloud.

items.headline: Beyond the blank slate: how Cloudflare accelerates your Zero Trust journey

content.excerpt_text: Project Helix simplifies and accelerates the onboarding process for Cloudflare One. By using automation and Terraform templates, this tool allows customers to quickly deploy a comprehensive, best-practice configuration in minutes.

items.headline: The truly programmable SASE platform

content.excerpt_text: As the only SASE platform with a native developer stack, we’re giving you the tools to build custom, real-time security logic and integrations directly at the edge.

items.headline: Toxic combinations: when small signals add up to a security incident

content.excerpt_text: Minor misconfigurations or request anomalies often seem harmless in isolation. But when these small signals converge, they can trigger a security incident known as a toxic combination. Here’s how to spot the signs.

items.headline: We deserve a better streams API for JavaScript

content.excerpt_text: The Web streams API has become ubiquitous in JavaScript runtimes but was designed for a different era. Here's what a modern streaming API could (should?) look like.

items.headline: Bringing more transparency to post-quantum usage, encrypted messaging, and routing security

items.headline: ASPA: making Internet routing more secure

content.excerpt_text: ASPA is the cryptographic upgrade for BGP that helps prevent route leaks by verifying the path network traffic takes. New features in Cloudflare Radar make tracking its adoption easy.

items.headline: How we rebuilt Next.js with AI in one week

items.headline: Cloudflare One is the first SASE offering modern post-quantum encryption across the full platform

items.headline: OpenClaw's shell env fallback trusts unvalidated SHELL path from host environment

content.excerpt_text: The shell environment fallback path could invoke an attacker-controlled shell when `SHELL` was inherited from an untrusted host environment. In affected builds, shell-env loading used `$SHELL -l -c 'env -0'` without validating that `SHELL` points to a trusted executable. In threat-model terms, this requires local environment compromise or untrusted startup environment injection first; it is not a remote pre-auth path. The hardening patch validates `SHELL` as an absolute normalized...

items.headline: OpenClaw's system.run approval TOCTOU via mutable symlink cwd target on node host

content.excerpt_text: ### Summary In `openclaw@2026.2.24`, approval-bound `system.run` on node hosts could be influenced by mutable symlink `cwd` targets between approval and execution. ### Details Approval matching on the gateway validated command/argv and binding fields, including `cwd`, as provided text. items.headline: OpenClaw's andbox browser noVNC observer lacked VNC authentication

content.excerpt_text: The sandbox browser entrypoint launched `x11vnc` without authentication (`-nopw`) for noVNC observer sessions.
